welcome_subtitle: A developer passionate about knowledge and UNIX™ systems.
about_title: Arthur de Souza Manske - 18 years old
about_text: I am a Full-Stack developer with vast experience in UNIX™ systems, kernel development, low-level libraries, file systems, and a high capacity for creating secure, high-performance web applications and APIs with clean architecture. Now, let's detail my skills:
projects_intro_title: And what have I done with that?
projects_intro_text: Below, you can read some of the most interesting projects I've produced with my skills and free time, because a programmer doesn't live only on words, but obviously also on programming!
usm_title: USM - Research Uniplexed Souza Manske Operating System
usm_text:
Operating system inspired by UNIX™ 7th Edition, modernized with support for x86 architectures, modern vulnerability mitigation, advanced scheduler, modern file systems, and ABI compatibility with Linux and FreeBSD.

Production mainly inspired by the primordial UNIX™ family; the development of this system was only possible thanks to MIT materials on XV6, version 1.0 of the LINUX™ operating system, and the availability of source codes at The UNIX™ Heritage Society.

For more information about the system or interest in contributing, please get in touch.
txfs_text: A UNIX™ style file system with native support for software-based dynamic RAID (similar to ZFS), journaling, snapshots, native encryption at the file system or file level, compression at the file system or file level, data deduplication, advanced integrity verification, subvolumes with different attributes and separate mounting possibility, support for extended attributes and, above all, it would offer advanced optimizations through the detection of the device type (specific optimizations for devices where fragmentation doesn't matter, SSDs with limited lifespan, among others).
posts_banner_text: Well, if you've made it this far, it's because you're interested, right? - Take a look, I'm always adding news
